texts.full_text: race against time to find solutions, they must also navigate the ethical dilemmas posed by this unique situation. The psychological toll on healthcare workers cannot be understated, as they battle fatigue, fear, and moral distress while witnessing countless tragedies firsthand. This crisis has brought to light the remarkable adaptability and resilience of both individuals and societies worldwide, forcing us all to confront our vulnerabilities and reconsider our priorities. Thanks to several decades of research mainly published in *Psychoneuroendocrinology,* the leading journal in the field, the psychoneuroendocrinology scientific community has acquired an incredible amount of knowledge on how stress is perceived, represented and embodied.
